project.name: consulting-revenue-platform-api
project.description: V2 backlog focused on architecture hardening, transactional integrity, and showcase-ready productization.

owner_profile_context.target_outcome: Portfolio-quality backend project that demonstrates direct business impact and production readiness.

execution.recommended_method: Implement one epic at a time in dependency order, preserving backwards-compatible API behavior while extracting clearer domain boundaries.
definition_of_done: All V2 stories for the active epic are marked done with acceptance criteria satisfied.
CI checks pass: fmt, unit tests, race tests, lint, migration checks, and build.
OpenAPI and runbook remain aligned with runtime behavior.
Critical money and workflow paths have explicit transaction-boundary tests.
global_constraints: All tenant-scoped business tables include organization_id.
All tenant-scoped queries enforce organization_id filtering.
Mutating endpoints require authentication and authorization.
Money values are stored as integer minor units; no floating-point arithmetic for currency.
All logs must avoid secrets and unnecessary PII.
Standard API error envelope is used everywhere.
Refactors must preserve existing route paths and auth expectations unless story scope explicitly states otherwise.

agent_rules: Do not proceed on a story until dependencies are complete.
Keep pull requests scoped to one story whenever practical.
For refactors, preserve behavior with targeted regression tests.
When uncertain, favor secure defaults and explicit validation.

categories.name: Domain and Use-Case Architecture
categories.description: Refactor critical business workflows into explicit domain/use-case layers while keeping API contracts stable.

stories.title: Define domain boundaries and interfaces for time-entry workflow
stories.description: Introduce explicit domain model and repository interfaces for time-entry lifecycle operations without changing external API contracts.
scope: Create domain package types for time-entry status and transition invariants.
Define repository interfaces required by time-entry submit, approve, and reject use cases.
Add architecture notes describing new package boundaries and dependency direction.
out_of_scope: Migrating invoicing logic in this story.
implementation_notes: Keep domain package free of database and HTTP imports.
Model transition guards as typed errors instead of string matching.
security_privacy: Role and tenant checks remain enforced before use-case execution.
edge_cases: Reject requires reason; preserve existing validation behavior.
acceptance_criteria: New domain and interface packages compile and are used by time-entry workflow path.
Behavior for submit/approve/reject endpoints is unchanged for success and error cases.
Tests verify domain transition matrix independent of HTTP transport.
test_plan: Add unit tests for domain transition rules.
Run existing HTTP authz and workflow tests to confirm no regression.
deliverables: Domain types and repository interfaces for time-entry lifecycle.
Short architecture doc update for dependency boundaries.

stories.title: Extract time-entry workflow into explicit application use-case layer
stories.description: Move time-entry submit, approve, and reject orchestration out of handlers into a dedicated use-case service.
scope: Implement application service for submit, approve, and reject flows.
Refactor handlers to parse/authorize and delegate to use-case layer.
Preserve audit event semantics for transition actions.
out_of_scope: Changing endpoint paths, auth headers, or status codes.
implementation_notes: Use constructor-based dependency injection for use-case service.
Map typed domain errors to existing HTTP error envelope in one adapter layer.
security_privacy: Authorization decisions stay in edge layer before use-case invocation.
edge_cases: Idempotent conflict responses remain consistent for already transitioned entries.
acceptance_criteria: Handlers for submit/approve/reject contain no direct repository transition calls.
All existing workflow endpoint tests pass unchanged or with only adapter-level updates.
Audit log actions remain emitted for successful transitions.
test_plan: Add use-case unit tests with in-memory or mock repositories.
Run internal/httpapi workflow and authz regression tests.
deliverables: Application use-case package for time-entry transitions.
Refactored handlers delegating to use-case service.

stories.title: Refactor invoice generation and send into use-case services with explicit transaction boundaries
stories.description: Consolidate invoice generation and send orchestration into use-case services and formalize transaction boundaries for multi-step writes.
scope: Introduce use-case services for invoice generate and send operations.
Use explicit transaction wrapper for multi-table invoice workflows.
Keep current response schema and route contract intact.
out_of_scope: Stripe payment-link and webhook reconciliation refactor.
implementation_notes: Make transaction ownership explicit in use-case layer, not handlers.
Preserve deterministic invoice totals and status transition semantics.
security_privacy: Tenant scoping and invoice write-role checks must remain enforced.
edge_cases: No eligible approved entries returns existing conflict behavior.
Send on non-draft invoice returns conflict without side effects.
acceptance_criteria: Invoice generate and send handlers delegate orchestration to use-case layer.
Transaction tests prove partial failure does not persist inconsistent invoice/time-entry state.
Existing endpoint response payload fields remain unchanged.
test_plan: Add integration tests for transaction rollback on injected failures.
Run current invoice API/authz tests and repo tests.
deliverables: Use-case service for invoice generation and send.

stories.title: Standardize typed domain errors and transport mapping across refactored flows
stories.description: Replace fragile string-based branching with typed errors and a centralized transport mapping layer for time-entry and invoice workflows.
scope: Define typed domain/application errors for conflict, validation, and not-found cases.
Add shared mapping helper from domain errors to API error envelope.
Apply mapping in refactored time-entry and invoice handlers.
out_of_scope: Global rewrite of every handler in the codebase.
implementation_notes: Use errors.Is-compatible sentinel/typed errors to avoid brittle substring checks.
Keep error code/message outputs stable for existing API consumers.
security_privacy: Do not expose internal stack or SQL details in client-facing errors.
edge_cases: Unknown errors still map to internal_error with request_id context.
Validation conflicts preserve expected HTTP status classes.
acceptance_criteria: Refactored handlers no longer branch on string-contains for expected business errors.
Error mapping tests cover known domain errors to HTTP code/code/message combinations.
External API error envelope format remains unchanged.
test_plan: Add table-driven tests for domain-to-transport error mapping.
Run existing endpoint regression tests for affected routes.
deliverables: Typed error definitions for refactored flows.
Shared transport error mapper used by handlers.

stories.title: Document architecture boundaries and contribution rules for domain/use-case/repo layers
stories.description: Add concise contributor guidance so future stories follow the new dependency direction and avoid relapsing into mixed-layer logic.
scope: Document layer responsibilities: transport, use-case, domain, repository.
Add concrete examples of where validation, authz, and transactions should live.
Reference these rules in README and runbook contribution sections.
out_of_scope: Organization-wide process docs outside this repository.
implementation_notes: Keep documentation actionable and under one page where possible.
Include migration guidance for remaining unrefactored handlers.
security_privacy: Document secure defaults for boundary placement (authz at transport edge, secrets never logged).
edge_cases: Contributors touching legacy paths need guidance to avoid half-refactors.
acceptance_criteria: Repository docs clearly define dependency direction and examples.
README includes a quick-start note for adding new use-case services.
Story J implementation areas are explicitly mapped to new boundaries.
test_plan: Manual documentation walkthrough validating that a new contributor can place code correctly.
Link check for any added internal docs references.
deliverables: Architecture guidance document for V2 layering model.
README/runbook updates referencing the new boundaries.
